title: Velvety Peppery Canadian Pistachio Curry
description: A velvety, peppery curry infused with toasted pistachios and charred bell peppers, served over tandoor-roasted cauliflower rice for a satisfying, low‑sugar Canadian twist.
instructions: Preheat your oven or tandoor to 250°C. Place the bell peppers on a baking sheet and roast until the skins blister and charred, about 10 minutes. Remove, cool, peel off skins, and slice into strips.
In a large skillet, heat the coconut oil over medium heat. Add the onion, garlic, and ginger; sauté until fragrant and the onion turns translucent, about 4 minutes.
Stir in cumin, coriander, smoked paprika, and cayenne pepper, cooking for 30 seconds to bloom the spices.
Add the diced tomatoes and bring to a gentle simmer. Cook for 8 minutes, allowing the sauce to thicken slightly.
Whisk in the light coconut milk and the toasted pistachios. Reduce heat to low and let the mixture simmer, stirring occasionally, for 6 minutes.
If using chicken, fold it in now and let the curry warm through for 2 minutes. Season with salt to taste.
Stir in the charred bell pepper strips and squeeze the lime juice over the curry. Remove from heat and let sit for 2 minutes to meld flavors.
Serve the curry over a bed of tandoor‑roasted cauliflower rice, garnish with fresh cilantro, and enjoy a velvety, peppery Canadian delight.
